title: Lexically-seeded PPR-fusion arm (the graph mechanism KEYSTONE)

tdd_evidence.red_failure: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'eval.m1_ppr' (module absent); then 2 substantive failures pre-fix (restart-collapse + IDF-live) when PageRank silently fell back to the seed vector on non-convergence

deliverables.deps_declared: src/python/pyproject.toml (scipy>=1.11, networkx>=3.0 in test+dev extras; harness-only, footprint-invariant safe)

load_bearing_properties_confirmed.AC-M1-15a_determinism: PASS — identical (query, passages, extractions) -> byte-identical ranking AND byte-identical passage_scores + ppr_mass (run twice, asserted equal).
load_bearing_properties_confirmed.AC-M1-15b_restart_1.0_collapse: PASS — at alpha=0.0 (teleport/restart=1.0) PageRank returns the personalization vector exactly: propagated (non-seed) entities have EXACTLY zero mass and ppr-only top-2 == BM25 seed passages; at alpha=0.85 those entities gain strictly positive mass (carol/dave: 0.0 -> 0.23 on the fixture). Propagation wired correctly.
load_bearing_properties_confirmed.AC-M1-15c_idf_weighting_live: PASS — hub-vs-specific fixture (two symmetric components): uniform weighting ties the two non-seed passages (carol==dave==0.214, index order) but IDF weighting flips them (dave 0.371 >> carol 0.032) -> the two ppr-only rankings differ ([0,2,3,1] vs [0,3,1,2]).
load_bearing_properties_confirmed.AC-M1-15d_fusion_no_regression: PASS — when BM25 and PPR both rank the seed passages above the rest and agree on their order, RRF (k=60) never drops a BM25-top-K passage below its BM25-alone rank (pr_g9-style pin).

ppr_config.idf_scheme: log((N+1)/(df+0.5)) over entity passage document-frequency; idf_weighting=False -> uniform 1.0 (ablation knob)
ppr_config.max_iter_tol_rationale: sized for near-bipartite entity-graph oscillation (decay rate alpha; ~85 iters needed for tol=1e-6 at alpha=0.85); a too-strict tol/too-few-iters silently fell back to the seed vector masking propagation -> caught RED->GREEN; fallback now retries at 10x budget, never silently collapses
ppr_config.membership: passage membership = the extracted 'entities' list only (deterministic); relation-only phrase nodes relay PPR mass but score no passage and can never be a seed
ppr_config.embedder: none — arm is lexical+graph only; CLS-pooling constraint does NOT apply

smoke.detail: Built ppr_fusion over 6 real questions (hops 2/3/4) from the pinned corpus + the Slice-10 preserved extractions (data/corpus-data/graph-cache/0.8.2-m1-v1/extractions.json, 300 qids). Per-question graphs: 115-277 entities, 115-282 edges; all 20 passages score nonzero; rankings are valid permutations of the pool.

cost_note: $0 — PPR runs in-harness (no API, no answerer, no priced extraction; the preserved Slice-10 graph is reused). The answerer is the only priced seam and belongs to Slice 20.
out_of_scope_respected: Did not touch the other arms (m1_baseline.retrieve_arms byte-identical), decide(), or any priced/answerer path. PPR is a parallel retrieve_ppr arm; add_ppr_fusion_arm is the Slice-20 hook.
tripwire: clean — git status --porcelain on canonical src/ dev/ tests/ empty before merge

self_check.pytest: 5 passed (test_m1_ppr.py); broader M1 suite 21 passed / 1 skipped
self_check.pyright: 0 errors, 0 warnings on eval/m1_ppr.py (resolved against the canonical .venv)
self_check.merge: --no-ff onto local main, clean (no file overlap with main's advance), no push/fetch/PR

next_step_for_orchestrator: ppr-fusion arm built + unit-verified; Slice 20 runs all 5 arms -> same answerer -> ΔF1 = (ppr-fusion) - (fused-RRF) at MATERIAL_F1_LIFT=0.04 -> GO/NO-GO via decide()
